# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

const env = Deno.env.toObject();

function getQueryModules(url: URL): string[] {
  const values = url.searchParams.getAll("module");
  const list: string[] = [];
  for (const value of values) {
    for (const part of value.split(",")) {
      const trimmed = part.trim();
      if (trimmed) list.push(trimmed);
    }
  }
  return list;
}

function getEnvModules(): string[] {
  const raw = env.PLUGIN_MODULES || env.MODULES || "";
  return raw
    .split(",")
    .map((s) => s.trim())
    .filter(Boolean);
}

function isAllowedModule(specifier: string): boolean {
  try {
    const u = new URL(specifier);
    return u.protocol === "https:" || u.protocol === "http:";
  } catch {
    return specifier.startsWith("./") || specifier.startsWith("../") || specifier.startsWith("/") || specifier.startsWith("file:");
  }
}

async function loadModule(specifier: string) {
  if (!isAllowedModule(specifier)) {
    throw new Error(`Disallowed module specifier: ${specifier}`);
  }
  return await import(specifier);
}

async function runPlugin(mod: any, context: Record<string, unknown>) {
  if (typeof mod?.default === "function") {
    return await mod.default(context);
  }
  if (typeof mod?.handler === "function") {
    return await mod.handler(context);
  }
  return mod;
}

Deno.serve(async (req: Request) => {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const modules = [...getEnvModules(), ...getQueryModules(url)];
  const context = {
    request: {
      method: req.method,
      url: req.url,
      headers: Object.fromEntries(req.headers.entries()),
    },
    env,
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
  };

  const results: Array<{ module: string; ok: boolean; result?: unknown; error?: string }> = [];

  for (const specifier of modules) {
    try {
      const mod = await loadModule(specifier);
      const result = await runPlugin(mod, context);
      results.push({ module: specifier, ok: true, result });
    } catch (error) {
      results.push({
        module: specifier,
        ok: false,
        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
      });
    }
  }

  const responseBody = {
    ok: true,
    modules,
    results,
  };

  return new Response(JSON.stringify(responseBody, null, 2), {
    headers: {
      "content-type": "application/json; charset=utf-8",
      "cache-control": "no-store",
    },
  });
});
